headline: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion to Micron, Intel, AMD in Q2
detail: This is the single biggest driver of your book today. A $2 trillion sector move in one quarter means your SMH, SOXL, and CRWD-adjacent names are riding genuine momentum, but also means the easy money may already be made. Crowded, extended rallies on margin are where the snapbacks are most painful.
rec: No new buys triggered by this - it's a reason to respect your stops more tightly, not to add exposure. If SMH pulls back cleanly to the $600 area you could revisit adding, but chasing a $2T Q2 run on margin is not the move today.

headline: Michael Burry shorting Caterpillar after AI-driven rally
detail: Burry making a high-profile short bet signals at least one smart money player thinks the AI-driven industrial rally is stretched. CAT isn't in your book but it speaks to a broader 'AI euphoria top' narrative that could spill into your chip names if sentiment shifts. Worth noting, not panicking over.
rec: No action on your holdings directly, but this is a yellow flag for the overall AI/tech sentiment trade. It reinforces keeping stops tight on SOXL and SPAL rather than loosening them into strength.

headline: ChatGPT connecting to bank accounts
detail: This is a real AI adoption signal - broader consumer integration of AI tools supports the secular demand story for chips and the infrastructure names in your portfolio. It's a slow-burn positive for CRWD (security implications) and the chip ecosystem generally.
rec: No trade here today - this is a long-term tailwind confirmation, not a near-term entry trigger. Sit with your existing CRWD position and let it work.

headline: Small-cap best start since 1991, but rest of 2026 may differ
detail: Your IWM scanner setup (7/12, Grade A) is interesting in this context - small caps ran hard but the headline itself warns the easy move may be done. IWM is not your core focus and adding small-cap exposure would dilute your chip concentration thesis.
rec: Skip the IWM scanner setup today - the headline directly flags that the small-cap momentum driver may be fading, and it doesn't fit your book. Focus stays on your chip and tech core.
